claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for your child.

Let me ask you some questions:

**What is your child's name and how old are they?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex and they're 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Now I need to understand your living situation and responsibility for Alex.

**Do you currently live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. Alex lives with me here in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming. Now I need to check a few more details about your circumstances.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are you or your partner (if you have one) a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status, or any other immigration status I should know about?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation, or have they been for an extended period recently?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital or care or anything like that. They just live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility requirements:

✓ Alex is under 16 years old (currently 8)
✓ You live with Alex in the UK
✓ You are responsible for Alex
✓ You are a UK citizen
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
✓ Alex is not in hospital or care

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. 

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you might need to pay back some or all of it through your tax return.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant meets all the key eligibility criteria: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (currently 8 years old), which satisfies the age requirement; (2) The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester), establishing they are responsible for the child; (3) The claimant is a UK citizen, satisfying the residency/citizenship requirements; (4) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, ensuring there is no duplicate claim; (5) Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation that would affect eligibility. All conditions for Child Benefit eligibility are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they are a UK citizen living in the UK with their child who is under 16, they are responsible for Alex, no one else is claiming for the child, and there are no special circumstances (such as hospitalization or care arrangements) that would affect eligibility.

All conditions for Child Benefit eligibility are met.

They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they are a UK citizen living in the UK with their child who is under 16, they are responsible for Alex, no one else is claiming for the child, and there are no special circumstances (such as hospitalization or care arrangements) that would affect eligibility.
